name: claude-legal-federal-laws
description: Shared reference corpus for federal U.S. debt-collection and consumer-finance law (FDCPA, FCRA, TILA, ECOA, Reg B/F/V/Z, plus RESPA, FHA, SCRA, and the FTC Telemarketing Sales Rule), the federal Bankruptcy Code (Title 11 — chapters 1, 3, 5, 7, 11, 12, 13, 15), the model Uniform Commercial Code (Articles 1, 2, 3, 9 — ALI/ULC model text), and the Americans with Disabilities Act with its core implementing regulations (ADA — 42 U.S.C. ch. 126; EEOC Title I reg 29 CFR Part 1630; DOJ Title II reg 28 CFR Part 35; DOJ Title III reg 28 CFR Part 36, incl. the 2010 ADA Standards for Accessible Design). Other plugins in the claude-legal marketplace declare this as a dependency and symlink into its `references/` tree so federal/UCC content lives in one place instead of being duplicated per state. The marketplace runtime auto-installs this plugin whenever a dependent state plugin is installed and dereferences the symlinks at install time, so each installed state plugin still gets the federal content locally. Also ships a nationwide, venue-independent consumer credit-report-rights skills layer built on the FCRA (15 U.S.C. §§ 1681 et seq.): consumer-report-ordering, consumer-credit-disputes, consumer-report-accuracy, consumer-harm-documentation, and consumer-credit-monitoring. These skills produce documents (request letters, dispute letters, identity-theft block requests, communication logs, damages ledgers, harm declarations, re-notification demands, review checklists), not legal advice, and are available anywhere a dependent state plugin is installed. Also ships an ada-rights skill — a nationwide, venue-independent ADA self-help layer that drafts reasonable-accommodation and reasonable-modification requests, ADA grievances, DOJ ADA complaints, and EEOC charge intake worksheets across ADA Title I (employment), Title II (state/local government), and Title III (public accommodations), citing the bundled ADA statute and DOJ/EEOC regulations. Bundles two free remote MCP servers in .mcp.json for on-demand case-law and statute lookup: CourtListener (https://mcp.courtlistener.com/ — Free Law Project's case-law / RECAP-docket / oral-argument / judges database; free account, OAuth sign-in on first use) and Legal Data Hunter (https://legaldatahunter.com/mcp — multi-jurisdictional statutes, case law, and doctrine; free, GitHub/Google sign-in). Because every state plugin depends on this plugin, the servers connect anywhere any state plugin is installed. A case-law-research skill drives them: it routes U.S. case law / RECAP dockets / judges to CourtListener and multi-jurisdictional or foreign law to Legal Data Hunter, and enforces the research discipline — snapshot-first for mirrored statutes and rules, never cite a case from memory, quote-check every holding against retrieved text, confidence-flag results, and hand drafts to the per-state fact-check skills.
